generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance CranePositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures the angular position of crane components (gantry, hoist, puck) with millisecond-level precision for real-time trajectory validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CranePositionData
]

instance CraneVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Captures linear and angular velocity of crane components using IMU-based sensors for dynamic model validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneVelocityData
]

instance LoadCellSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors payload forces and torque distribution to detect overloading or mechanical stress anomalies."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LoadForceData
]

instance ActuatorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Tracks motor currents to identify actuator failures or energy consumption patterns for optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ActuatorCurrentData
]

instance LimitSwitchSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Detects physical constraints (e.g., end-of-travel stops) to prevent collisions and enforce operational boundaries."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LimitSwitchData
]

instance EnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures ambient conditions (temperature, humidity) affecting crane operation in laboratory environments."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance CranePositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CranePositionEncoder
    base:desc "Transmits joint angles (gantry, hoist, puck) at millisecond intervals via OPC UA protocol for real-time trajectory synchronization."
]

instance CraneVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneVelocitySensor
    base:desc "Streams velocity vectors with sub-millisecond timestamps for dynamic model validation and control feedback."
]

instance LoadForceData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LoadCellSensor
    base:desc "Sends force measurements (N/m) at 1kHz sampling rate for payload stability assessment and fault detection."
]

instance ActuatorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ActuatorCurrentSensor
    base:desc "Transmits motor current profiles (A) for energy optimization and torque anomaly detection via MQTT broker."
]

instance LimitSwitchData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LimitSwitchSensor
    base:desc "Binary state signals (ON/OFF) for safety-critical boundary enforcement with 1ms latency."
]

instance EnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensor
    base:desc "Periodic environmental readings (1Hz) stored for operational context analysis in TimescaleDB."
]

instance TrajectoryExecutionLog : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CranePositionEncoder, CraneVelocitySensor
    base:desc "Timestamped records of executed waypoints, control signals, and actuator commands for post-mortem validation."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Forward/inverse kinematic solver implemented in MATLAB/Simulink for trajectory planning and collision avoidance."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KinematicSimulationEnabler, TrajectoryOptimizationEnabler
]

instance DynamicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based model (PyBullet) simulating crane dynamics including payload interactions and actuator delays."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DynamicValidationEnabler, ControlParameterAdaptationEnabler
]

instance ControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "PID/MPC controller implementation with adaptive gain scheduling for real-time trajectory tracking."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeControlEnabler
]

instance HistoricalOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured time-series logs (PostgreSQL) of crane operations including positions, velocities, forces, and timestamps."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CranePositionData, CraneVelocityData, LoadForceData, TrajectoryExecutionLog
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TrendAnalysisEnabler, AnomalyDetectionEnabler, ComparativeVisualizationEnabler
]

instance RealTimeSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Low-latency sensor fusion dataset (50ms delay) for closed-loop control and validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CranePositionData, CraneVelocityData, ActuatorCurrentData, LimitSwitchData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContinuousValidationServiceEnabler
]

]

    base:desc "Machine learning-trained patterns (Isolation Forests, LSTM autoencoders) for mechanical fault classification."

]

// C11: Enablers

    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink environment for forward/inverse kinematic calculations with 10ms computation time."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeTrajectoryOptimizationService
]

]

]

]

]

]

]

]

]

]

    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]

]

    base:desc "Python-based adaptive PID gain scheduler using real-time sensor feedback."

]

]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeTrajectoryOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Computes and enforces optimal motion profiles minimizing energy consumption or maximizing throughput using kinematic/dynamic models."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalTrajectoryInsight, EnergyOptimizationAction
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the Digital Twin system is mediated by the **Control Application** and **Trajectory Generator Service**, which translate digital commands into physical actions for the **Physical Crane**. Key aspects include:

**Command Types**:
- **Trajectory commands**: Generated by the **Trajectory Generator Service** using a kinematics model to produce optimal movement profiles (e.g., spline-based paths) with zero residual swinging at termination.
- **Configuration commands**: Sent via the **Human Machine Interface (HMI)** to adjust crane parameters (e.g., speed limits, safety zones).
- **Emergency stop commands**: Triggered manually or via the **Continuous Validation Service** if physical-virtual discrepancies exceed thresholds.

**Control Message Formats**:
- **Structured payloads** (likely JSON or binary) containing:
  - **Waypoints**: Cartesian or joint-space coordinates.
  - **Timestamps**: For synchronized execution.
  - **Velocity/acceleration profiles**: Derived from the kinematics model.
- **Command validation**: The **Control Application** verifies trajectory feasibility against physical constraints (e.g., crane limits) before transmission.

**Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
- **Trajectory validation**: The **Continuous Validation Service** compares executed trajectories (logged data) with simulated trajectories (from the **Simulation Service**) using distance metrics (e.g., Euclidean error). Threshold breaches trigger alerts via the **Dashboard**.
- **Authentication**: Not explicitly detailed, but implied for critical commands (e.g., HMI-to-Control Application communication).

**Feedback Systems**:
- **Real-time telemetry**: The **Physical Crane Interface** relays execution feedback (e.g., actual vs. commanded position) to the **Logging Application** and **Validation Service**.
- **Conflict resolution**: Not explicitly described, but the **Control Application** likely prioritizes commands based on urgency (e.g., emergency stops override trajectory commands).

**Command Execution Confirmation**:
- **Acknowledgment signals**: The **Physical Crane Interface** confirms receipt/execution of commands (mechanism unspecified but inferred via logging).
- **Post-execution validation**: The **Validation Service** generates alerts if discrepancies exceed thresholds, enabling corrective action via the **Dashboard**.
"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system exemplars adopt a **lightweight, ad-hoc orchestration pattern** without reliance on commercial frameworks. **GreenhouseDT** uses a **hybrid architecture** combining **InfluxDB (time-series database)** for data persistence, **ActiveMQ (message broker)** for asynchronous communication, and **virtual machines** for deployment, enabling **self-adaptive behavioral synchronization** between the physical greenhouse and its digital twin. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar employs a **pipeline-based orchestration** on **Microsoft Azure**, integrating **AutomationML** for model-driven twin representation and a **Raspberry Pi** for sensor data acquisition. Both systems lack centralized orchestration engines; instead, they rely on **containerization/virtualization** (e.g., virtual machines, Raspberry Pi) and **event-driven coordination** (via message brokers or direct sensor-DT links). The **gantry crane** exemplar follows a similar pattern, using **C4 Model** for architectural visualization but does not specify orchestration details beyond ad-hoc integration."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar emphasizes **self-adaptive behavioral models** for both the physical greenhouse and its digital twin, implying dynamic validation through real-time adjustments. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar focuses on **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction**, suggesting validation via **model-driven engineering (AutomationML)** and **Microsoft Azure-based analytics**. Uncertainty quantification and error correction mechanisms are not explicitly detailed, but the use of **prerecorded data in virtual machines (GreenhouseDT)** and **Azure tools (Air Quality Management)** implies structured data management for quality assurance. No explicit mention of **uncertainty sources, error detection protocols, or formal verification methods** (e.g., statistical validation, cross-model comparison) is provided in the documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s technical network infrastructure** is designed to support real-time interaction between physical and virtual components, with the following specifications:

**Communication Protocols**:
- **MQTT**: Used as a lightweight, publish-subscribe broker for inter-service signaling (e.g., alerting the **Dashboard** of validation failures or trajectory completion).
- **Database protocols**: **TimescaleDB** supports high-throughput writes (optimized for time-series data) and likely uses PostgreSQL’s native protocols for client-server communication.
- **Physical interface protocols**: Unspecified, but the **Physical Crane Interface** (Python-based) likely uses serial (e.g., RS-232/485), Ethernet/IP, or proprietary protocols to communicate with crane controllers.

**Network Topology and Architecture**:
- **Centralized**: Services (e.g., **Trajectory Generator**, **Validation Service**) communicate via the **MQTT Broker** and **TimescaleDB**, implying a star topology with the database as a central hub.
- **Service isolation**: Containers (e.g., **Control Application**, **Logging Application**) are logically separated but interconnected via shared data stores and messaging.

**Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
- **Low-latency paths**: Critical for real-time control (e.g., trajectory execution) and validation (e.g., comparing logged vs. simulated data). Latency targets are not quantified but inferred to be <100ms for control loops.
- **High-throughput logging**: **TimescaleDB** must handle continuous writes from the **Logging Application** (unspecified volume but likely kilobytes/second for sensor data).

**Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
- **Database redundancy**: **TimescaleDB** may include replication or backups (not detailed).
- **Service resilience**: The **Validation Service** and **Dashboard** rely on continuous data streams; failures may trigger alerts but no explicit failover is described.
- **MQTT QoS**: Likely configured for at-least-once delivery to ensure critical alerts (e.g., threshold breaches) are not lost.

**Security Protocols**:
- **Not explicitly detailed**, but implied measures may include:
  - **Authentication**: For HMI access or critical command paths.
  - **Encryption**: For data in transit (e.g., MQTT over TLS, database connections).
  - **Access control**: Role-based (e.g., **Operator** vs. **Researcher** access to the **Dashboard**).
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s hosting and deployment infrastructure** is tailored to the lab-scale gantry crane system, with the following technical specifics:

**Hosting Platforms**:
- **On-premise**: The system is deployed locally (implied by the lab-scale context and lack of cloud references), likely on a single machine or small cluster for low-latency operation.
- **Edge deployment**: The **Physical Crane Interface** and **Control Application** run on hardware co-located with the crane to minimize latency.

**Computational and Storage Requirements**:
- **Database**: **TimescaleDB** requires sufficient RAM and disk I/O for time-series data storage (unspecified capacity but optimized for high-write workloads).
- **Services**:
  - **Trajectory Generator**: Computationally intensive (kinematics modeling) but likely lightweight for lab-scale operations.
  - **Simulation Service**: Requires real-time processing for trajectory validation.
  - **MQTT Broker**: Low-resource overhead for local messaging.
- **Frontend**: The **Dashboard (Grafana)** and **HMI** are likely hosted on the same machine or a separate workstation for operator interaction.

**Scalability Mechanisms**:
- **Vertical scaling**: Implied for the database (e.g., increasing RAM/disk for larger datasets).
- **Service modularity**: Containers (e.g., **Validation Service**) can be scaled independently if deployed in a containerized environment (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes), though not explicitly detailed.

**Deployment Models Supported**:
- **Monolithic or microservices**: The C4 container diagram suggests a microservices-like architecture (e.g., **Trajectory Generator** as a standalone service), but deployment specifics (e.g., orchestration) are not described.
- **Hybrid**: Physical components (crane hardware) are standalone, while digital twin services are software-based and co-located.

**Availability and Reliability Assurance**:
- **Continuous operation**: Critical services (e.g., **Logging Application**, **Validation Service**) must run persistently to ensure real-time data capture and validation.
- **Fault tolerance**: Not explicitly detailed, but implied redundancy in data storage (e.g., **TimescaleDB** backups) and service monitoring (e.g., **Dashboard** alerts).
- **Maintenance**: The system is designed for research and operational use, with no explicit high-availability (HA) clustering or disaster recovery (DR) mechanisms described.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems demonstrate **limited horizontal integration** with external systems, relying on **domain-specific, lightweight protocols** rather than standardized interoperability frameworks. **GreenhouseDT** integrates with **InfluxDB** (for data storage) and **ActiveMQ** (for messaging), but no explicit external system dependencies (e.g., ERP, IoT platforms) are mentioned. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar uses **Microsoft Azure tools** for pipeline implementation, suggesting potential integration with **cloud-based services** (e.g., Azure IoT Hub, Logic Apps), but no concrete protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) or enterprise system dependencies are documented. Both exemplars avoid commercial Digital Twin frameworks, opting for **ad-hoc sensor-DT links** (e.g., Raspberry Pi sensors → AutomationML twin). The **gantry crane** exemplar does not specify external integrations but implies **cyber-physical system (CPS) alignment** with logistics domains, potentially requiring **standardized CPS protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, ROS) for broader interoperability, though these are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The **Air Quality Management** exemplar adheres to **AutomationML** ([ISO 15926-compliant](https://www.automationml.org/)) for digital twin modeling, ensuring interoperability with industrial engineering tools. The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar does not explicitly mention standardization but relies on **open-source technologies** (InfluxDB, ActiveMQ) and **virtualization** (VMs), which are widely adopted in IoT and CPS domains. Neither exemplar references **certification requirements** or **compliance frameworks** (e.g., ISO 23247 for Digital Twins, IEC 62443 for cybersecurity). **Interoperability** is limited to internal toolchains (e.g., AutomationML for Air Quality Management), with no evidence of cross-domain standards (e.g., OPC UA, FIWARE) for broader integration."
]
